title: FID: Comparing Distributions

exercises.explain: Before we dive into FID, remember that IS focuses on quality and diversity of single images, while FID will compare whole groups of images.
exercises.prompt: What does the Inception Score (IS) we learned about earlier primarily measure?

options.text: It measures how clear and diverse the generated images are on their own.
It compares the generated images directly to a set of real target images.
It calculates the exact pixel difference between two identical pictures.

cards.body: Imagine you generated thousands of synthetic cat photos. How do you prove they don't just look okay individually, but actually match the variety and style of a real set of cats? You need a way to compare the whole group!
cards.title: Comparing Crowds
What is FID?
cards.body: Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) is a metric that measures the similarity between a set of real images and a set of generated images. It extracts deep features using a pre-trained neural network (Inception) and calculates the distance between their mathematical distributions.

Think of real images as a group of professional dancers and fake images as students. Instead of comparing them dancer-by-dancer, you compare the overall shape, energy, and spread of the two groups on the dance floor. If the two crowds look and move similarly, the 'distance' between their styles is very small.
cards.title: The Dance Floor Comparison

cards.body: If you run a real dataset of 10,000 dog photos through the Inception network, you get a profile of their features. You do the same for 10,000 AI-generated dogs. If the average features and their variations are almost identical, the FID score will be very close to 0.
cards.title: Measuring Dog Profiles
Lower is Actually Better
cards.body: You might think a higher FID score is better because 'higher' usually means more. But FID is a distance! A lower FID means the distance between real and fake is smaller, meaning your AI model is performing much better.

cards.title: Low vs High FID

left.text: The generated images share almost the same colors, shapes, and variety as the real set.

right.text: The generated images are blurry, distorted, or lack the variety of the real set.

cards.body: Let's look at how FID values translate to visual similarity between the datasets.

options.text: A higher score is always better.
A lower score means the generated images are closer to the real ones.
It only checks if the images are high resolution, ignoring variety.

exercises.explain: FID measures the distance between the distribution of real and generated image features. A lower distance means the two sets are more similar.
exercises.prompt: Which of the following is true about the Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) score?
True or False: FID requires a reference set of real images to compare against the generated images.
exercises.explain: FID compares the feature distributions of two sets of images, not just single images in isolation.
Since FID measures mathematical distance, a lower value means the generated images are closer to the real distribution.
exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence about evaluating GANs.
exercises.sentence: When comparing two GAN models, the one with the [blank] FID score is generally considered to produce higher quality and more realistic images.

options.text: System A (FID = 85.0) because higher scores indicate superior creativity.
System B (FID = 12.0) because a lower score indicates the output closely matches real images.

exercises.prompt: Identify which system is producing more realistic and diverse images based on their FID scores:
exercises.explain: The second system has a much lower FID (12.0 vs 85.0), indicating its output distribution is much closer to the real image distribution.

options.text: The generated images are completely different from the real images.
The generated images are completely black and white.
The generated distribution is identical to the real distribution.
The AI model has failed to learn anything.

exercises.explain: An FID of 0.0 means the two distributions are identical, representing a perfect match between the generated and real image feature distributions. In practice, this is extremely rare for generated images but represents the ideal target. Next, we will explore conditional GAN architectures.
exercises.prompt: What does an FID score of 0.0 theoretically represent?

concept: Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) measures the similarity of deep features between real and fake image sets; lower distance means the fake distribution is closer to the real distribution.
